title: Astracantha echidna
intro:
There is no plant called Astracantha echidna. However, Astragalus (Astragalus spp.) is a genus of plants that includes species with similar properties. 

Astragalus is a type of flowering plant, also known as milk vetch or goat's thorn, commonly found in Asia and North America. The health benefits of Astragalus include its ability to boost the immune system, reduce inflammation, and improve cardiovascular health. 

The bioactive constituents of Astragalus include flavonoids, saponins, and polysaccharides. These constituents are believed to contribute to the plant's antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and immunomodulatory effects.

The most common herbal preparations made with Astragalus include teas, tinctures, and capsules. Astragalus is often used in traditional Chinese medicine to enhance vitality and support overall well-being.
